summary: McKenzie reports on the results of changing the icons/buttons on his Bingo Card Creator website. The new download button saw a 1% decrease in clicks (from 9.5% to 8.5%), but text links compensated to keep overall conversion steady. The Buy Now button saw a significant increase from 1% to 3% click-through. He notes uncertainty about whether the improvement came from the visual redesign or the copy change from 'Purchase Now' to 'Buy Now!'
key_points: New download trial button decreased clicks by ~1%, but text links picked up the slack keeping overall conversion constant
Buy Now button clicks tripled from 1% to 3%, a significant improvement
He can't isolate whether the gain came from the visual redesign or the copy change from 'Purchase Now' to 'Buy Now!'
This reflects early-stage A/B testing habits before he had more rigorous split-testing infrastructure
money_quotes: 2% more people (1 -> 3) are clicking the Buy Now! button.
I'm not entirely sure whether that is a result of just the redesigned buttons or the result of changing Purchase Now to Buy Now! but either way, what can I say, I like it.
key_insight: Small changes to button design and copy can meaningfully move conversion metrics, though isolating which variable caused the change requires more disciplined testing.
